def: s.m. = ḍāṅthal, q.v.
s.f. Tooth (of a saw, &c.); cog (of a wheel); — a row of teeth; — lockjaw; — a sickle; a rake; a harrow; — a notch, dent, jag
subs: (-meṅ), To be notched or indented, to become jagged
(-ko), To make (one) hold his (or her) tongue; to snub; — to insist upon
v.n. The teeth to be clenched; to have lockjaw.

def: s.f. A medicinal plant yielding a pungent oil, Croton polyandrum, or C. tigtium
subs: s.m. A strongly purgative nut, the fruit of the croton.

def: adj. & s.m. Having teeth, toothed; having large or projecting teeth, tusked; serrated, notched, jagged; (in Bot.) erose, sinuate; — an elephant; a boar
subs: s.m. A toothed or cogged wheel.

def: adj. Dental, of or belonging to the teeth, produced on the teeth; — s.m. The dental letters
adj. Made of an elephant's tusk, or of ivory; ivory.
s.m. A mixture of an alkali (called reh) and tobacco (used for the teeth); tooth-powder.
v.t. = ḍāṭnā, q.v.
v.n. To get teeth (said of cattle, horses, &c.).
s.m.f. Having projecting teeth; — a person with projecting teeth.
s.m.f. A person whose front teeth project.
s.m. = daṅtāʼolī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Having long or projecting teeth, tusked; toothed; — an elephant a boar, or other animal that has large or prominent tusks.
s.m. = dāṅtan; datwan, q.v.
adj. Dental, of or belonging to the teeth, produced on the teeth; — s.m. The dental letters
s.f. Name of one of the daughters of Daksha, wife of Kaśyapa, and mother of the demons called Dānavas (the Titans of Hindū mythology)
subs: vulg. danuj, s.m. (f. -ā), A son of Danu, a Dānava or demon.

def: s.m. See s.v. دنو danu.

ety: S. द्रवं or द्रव्यं, rt. द्रु; — P. dāv or daʼo
def: s.m. Time, turn; vicissitude; opportunity, chance; (fig.) power, clutch, grasp ( = qābū); — ambuscade, ambush ( = ghāt); snare, trap, stratagem, artifice, trick, manœuvre; sleight or trick (in wrestling); — a stake, wager; throw or cast (of dice); stroke (at a game)
subs: v.n. To lie in ambush, to lurk
v.t. To bring under (one's) power; to get a grip (of one's opponent in wrestling)
dāṅʼo-par lagānā or lagā-denā, v.t. To stake, wager
v.n. To be thrown (dice); to throw high, have a good throw
(apnā), To watch (one's) opportunity; to lie in wait (for)
To make the most of an opportunity, to take advantage
v.n. To have the advantage; — dāṅʼo denā (-ko), To give (one) his turn; to trick, deceive
To practise a sleight or trick (in wrestling)
(-se), To be tricked, be taken in
v.n. To get an opportunity or chance
v.n. To take (one's) turn; — to seize an opportunity

def: adj. = H ڐانواڐولی डांवाडोली ḍāṅwā-ḍolī, s.f. see ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, and ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī.

r: ḍaṅwāḍol, ḍaṅwāḍolī, ḍaṅwāṅḍol, ḍaṅwāṅḍolī, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī
def: = H ڐنواڐولی डंवाडोली ḍaṅwāḍolī, = H ڐنوانڐول डंवांडोल ḍaṅwāṅḍol, = H ڐنوانڐولي डंवांडोली ḍaṅwāṅḍolī See ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, and ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī.
s.f. = H ڐانواڐول डांवाडोल ḍāṅwā-ḍol, adj. see ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, and ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī.

r: ḍaṅwāḍolī, ḍaṅwāḍol, ḍaṅwāṅḍol, ḍaṅwāṅḍolī, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī
def: = H ڐنواڐول डंवाडोल ḍaṅwāḍol, = H ڐنوانڐول डंवांडोल ḍaṅwāṅḍol, = H ڐنوانڐولي डंवांडोली ḍaṅwāṅḍolī See ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, and ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī.
s.m. A forest conflagration; burning stubble, a bonfire.
adj. = ḍāwāṅ, q.v.

adj. Wandering without house or home, errant; unsettled, uneasy, restless, distressed (in mind); ruined, lost, destitute; — s.m. A running or hurrying to and fro; a convulsion (as of an earthquake, &c.)
subs: v.n. To go wandering about, to be unsettled, &c.

r: ḍaṅwāṅḍol, ḍaṅwāḍol, ḍaṅwāḍolī, ḍaṅwāṅḍolī, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī
def: = H ڐنواڐول डंवाडोल ḍaṅwāḍol, = H ڐنواڐولی डंवाडोली ḍaṅwāḍolī, = H ڐنوانڐولي डंवांडोली ḍaṅwāṅḍolī See ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, and ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī.
s.f. The wandering about without house or home, unsettled or restless state; state of destitution.

r: ḍaṅwāṅḍolī, ḍaṅwāḍol, ḍaṅwāḍolī, ḍaṅwāṅḍol, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī
def: = H ڐنواڐول डंवाडोल ḍaṅwāḍol, = H ڐنواڐولی डंवाडोली ḍaṅwāḍolī, = H ڐنوانڐول डंवांडोल ḍaṅwāṅḍol, See ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, and ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍolī.
adj. = ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, q.v.

ety: Prk. दामडिआ; S. दाम+र+इका; see dām
def: s.f. A rope, &c. ( = dām); the rope with which bullocks are tied together when treading out corn.

ety: ḍaṅw˚ = ḍāw˚ = ḍāb(nā), q.v.+r = ar = Prk. अड = S. र+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A tiger's cub or whelp.
s.m. = dāhinā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) 1˚ = dāʼo; — 2˚ = ḍāh; qq.v.
s.m. Stratagem, feint, &c. ( = dāṅw, q.v.)

ety: S. दात्रं (rt. दा 'to cut'); P. dāv or dāʼo
def: s.m. A kind of hatchet with a hooked point, a bill; a sickle.
s.m. = dāṅʼo, q.v.

ety: Prk. दप्पो; S. दर्पः, rt. दृप्
def: s.m. Pride, arrogance, haughtiness, insolence; self-importance, vanity, conceit; boastfulness.
s.m. 1˚ = ḍab, 'leather,' &c.; — 2˚ = ḍaph, qq.v.
s.m. Stamping forcibly; — strength, potency.
s.m. = ḍaphālī, q.v.

ety: Prk. दबडी; S. द्रव+ट or र+ई; cf. dauṛ(nā)
def: s.f. Course, gallop; rush; attack (com. used in comp. with dauṛ).

ety: i.q. ḍauṛnā, q.v.; ḍapaṭ = Prk. dim. n. दवड
def: v.n. To run, rush, gallop; to call out, shout, bawl; — v.t. To shout at, challenge, rebuke, menace; to urge (a horse); to rush at, spring upon, attack, assault.
v.n. To gallop, to rush; — v.t. To rebuke, reprimand; to menace; to attack ( = ḍāṅṭnā).
s.f. Shining, brilliance, splendour.

ety: dapdap˚, prob. = S. दर्प˚ (rt. दृप् 'to light') redupl.
def: v.n. To shine
s.m. A tambourine.
s.m. A tambourine player, a drummer, &c. ( = dafālī); — (met.) a chatterer.
v.n. To make a great noise, to cry aloud or bitterly, to blubber, to roar.
s.m. A kind of tambourine or tabor.
s.m. = ḍabkā, q.v.

ety: Prk. डप्पउल्लो or दप्पउल्लो; S. डप् or डम्प (prob. for दृप्)+ उलः
def: adj. Big, large, huge, monstrous, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. lit. A huge conch-shell; making a great noise but effecting nothing, promising much and performing nothing; — one who talks loud but does nothing, &c.

ety: Prk. डप्पइउओ; S. उम्पयितृ+कः, rt. डम्प्, prob. fr. दृप्
def: s.m. A large or huge object; a big, fat man; a monster.

ety: daf, q.v.+rā = arā = Prk. अडओ = S. अ+र+कः
def: s.m. = ḍaphlā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to gallop, &c.; to gallop.
v.t. To ride or gallop hard; to urge (a horse) impetuously (against, -par), to charge with impetuosity (a horseman).
s.f. The anus (vulgar).

ety: fr. dāshtan; rt. Zend dar = S. धर्
def: s.m. Taking, seizing, capturing; capture; — entanglement, difficulty; — tumult, conflict
subs: dār o madār, s.m. Agreement, stipulation, contract; adjustment of a dispute, conciliation; reconciliation; dependence; protection, safeguard.

def: s.m. Wood, a piece of wood; gallows, gibbet; impaling stake
s.m. (but in A. f. & m.), Abode, dwelling, habitation, house, mansion, seat; country, district
subs: s.m. House or abode of safety; a country with which there is peace
The everlasting abode, the next world
s.m. The abode of perdition, hell
s.m. 'Land of war'; country of an enemy, or of infidels, on whom it is lawful to make war
dāruʼl-ḵẖilāfat, s.m. Seat of empire or government, royal residence, capital
s.m. Abode of peace or safety, heaven
A court of law, a spiritual court
The abode of perishableness, the perishable abode, the world
The abode of stability, the stable abode; the mansion of rest; a burial-ground; the grave; one of the seven heavens
The abode of recompense or retaliation, the world

def: s.f. = dāl, q.v.
s.m. Door, gate; — adj. & adv. (in comp.) Outer; out
subs: v.n. To come out, to issue
adv. From door to door; — s.m. One who goes from door to door, mendicant, vagrant
s.f. The going from door to door; begging, vagrancy
s.m. Door-fastening, bolt or bar of a door
adv. = dar-ba-bar, q.v.; — dar-dar māṅgnā, To beg from door to door
To look anxiously around, to be in anxious expectation; to feel anxiety and solicitude.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian در (dar). Doublet of دوار (dvār).

ety: for an-dar; Pehl. dar; Zend añtare; S. antar
def: s.m. Under-tenure or farm, sub-lease
subs: s.m. Holder of an under-tenure, a sub-farmer of revenue

s.f. A coming in, going in, ingress, entrance; access; arrival; income, receipt; import; account of fees paid for serving processes, the return of a process; — perf. part. (contrac. of dar-āmada), Come in; — dar-āmad-bar-āmad, s.f. Ingress and egress; receipts and disbursements; — dar-āmad honā (-meṅ), To come or go (into), to enter; to arrive, &c.
v.n. To come in, to enter; to penetrate; to arrive; to enter (upon, -meṅ), engage (in); to be successful, to succeed (see also s.v. dar, 'door')
(-ě), adv. In the matter (of), relating (to), respecting, in respect (of), as (to)
adj. & adv. Concealed, veiled, hidden, secret; — secretly, privately, in private; in disguise, by innuendo, by implication, indirectly
dar-paʼe, adv. & postpn. In the footsteps (of, -ke), following, after, close behind; in pursuit or quest (of); in prosecution (of), intent (on)
(-kisī-ke), To seek or desire the death (of anyone), to persecute to the uttermost

(-ke), To be in the track (of), to be after, be in pursuit (of), to follow, to pursue; to be in quest or search (of), to seek; to be close at the heels (of), be at hand; to dog the steps (of), to persecute; to prosecute, to be intent (on)
adv. In front (of, -ke), before; on the tapis; under consideration, under trial; — dar-pesh karnā, v.t. To place or lay before, to introduce, bring forward, adduce
(-ke), To be in front (of), be before; to be on the carpet or anvil; to be in hand, be on foot; be pending; to happen, occur; to be incumbent, be necessary
(-ko), To bring forward, adduce ( = dar-pesh karnā)
s.f. The being before (a court at law)
adv. At the present time, at present, just now, immediately, on the instant, on the spot
dar ḥālate-ki, adv. In case that, in the event of; under the circumstances
s.m. Hem, or embroidery, or ornament, of a skirt, or of a gown or robe
adv. In case, in the event (of), provided, supposing (that, -ki); since, inasmuch as
adv. On one side, apart; out of the way, aside; out of the question; — put it aside! — dar-gāh, See s.v.
s.m. Passing by or over, overlooking, excusing, &c.; — dar-guẕar karnā (-se), To pass by or over, to overlook, excuse, forgive; to turn aside (from); to neglect (see next)
(-se), To pass by or over, &c. ( = dar-guẕar karnā); to leave off, desist (from), abstain or refrain (from), to decline
s.f. Catching, taking, &c.; — dar-girift karnā, v.t. To catch, seize, take; to captivate
intj. (colloq.) (May you lie) in the grave! may you die! (used by women)
adj. Taking hold of, making an impression, effective; burning, inflaming
v.t. To bring in or under; to include, comprise
s.f. Misery, distress, wretchedness, penury, misfortune
part. adj. Destitute, distressed, ill at ease, miserable, wretched; helpless, without remedy
dar-māhā, dar-māha, dar-mahā, s.m. Monthly wages, salary, pay; — adv. per mensem, monthly
dar-māhe-dār, s.m. One who receives monthly wages
adv. At this time, at present; in this case; by these presents.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian در (dar). Doublet of دوار (dvār).

ety: prob. fr. S. दृ; cf. S. ā-dar
def: s.f. A statement of the different rates of a village; rent rates used for assessments; assessing the price or value of crops or produce
subs: s.f. (in Arith.) The rule of Practice.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian در (dar). Doublet of دوار (dvār).

def: s.f. Line, row, rank, range, string (as of deer, birds, &c.)
subs: A whole line or row or string, a whole herd, a whole flock.

def: s.f. = ḍāl, q.v.

ety: Prk. डरो or दरो; S. दरः
def: adj. & s.m. 'Entertaining fear,' timid, &c. ( = ḍarāk); — a timid person; a coward
subs: adv. By intimidation; — ḍar dikhānā, v.t. To frighten, put in dread (of, -kā), to intimidate, to scare

ety: fr. dar-īdan; rt. Zend dar = S. dara, rt. दृ
def: part. adj. Rending, tearing (used in comp., e.g. mardum-dar).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian در (dar). Doublet of دوار (dvār).

ety: P. dara; S. दर+कः, rt. दृ
def: s.m. The highway; — s.m.f. = darra, and darī, qq.v.
s.m. Holder, possessor, lord; king, sovereign; god; — prop. n. Darius.
s.m. Crack, crevice, fissure, breach, a hole (to plant a tree, or to fix a post in); a cavity, cave; shell.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian در (dar). Doublet of دوار (dvār).

def: s.f. A wife
subs: (dāra+adh˚), adj. & s.m. Dependent on, or subject to, a wife; — a hen-pecked husband; a husband who depends on his wife.

def: s.f. A wife
subs: (dāra+adh˚), adj. & s.m. Dependent on, or subject to, a wife; — a hen-pecked husband; a husband who depends on his wife.

ety: Zend dar; S. दर, rt. दृ
def: s.m. A valley (especially between hills, through which a stream flows), a glen; a mountain-pass, a defile; — a crack, fissure, &c. ( = dar and darā).

ety: said to be comp. of dar, 'in,' and ā, 'come'
def: s.f. A bell ( = dar-āʼī).
prop. n. Name of a Persian king (the father of Darius, and the eighth king of the Kaiānian dynasty).
s.m. A kind of poison (brought from the country called Darad).
s.m. pl. of dirham, q.v.
adj. Of or belonging to Darius; — s.f. Sovereignty; — red silk cloth (vulg. daryāʼī).

r: darāʼī, dar-āʼe, dar, āʼī, āʼī-dan, ā, i
ety: dar, 'in'+āʼī, rt. of āʼī-dan, fr. ā+rt. Zend i, 'to go'; = S. i
def: s.f. 'Coming in, entering '; commencing a discourse; beginning or exordium of a discourse, or oration; talking, conversation (used in comp., e.g. dīda-darāʼī, s.f. Intercourse or conversation with the eyes; — harza-daraʼī, s.f. Talking nonsense, chattering); — breaking in upon conversation, interrupting; contradicting; — a bell ( = darā): — a blacksmith's hammer.
s.m. 'The two worlds,' the present and future life.
s.f. = dalaitī, q.v.

ety: for A. درجة darajat, fem., v.n. fr. درج 'to go step by step'
def: s.m. A stair, step, stage, story, degree (in progress, &c.), grade, order, degree, station, rank, dignity; position, situation, plight; a degree (of a circle); an act (of a play)
subs: adj. Step by step, by degrees, gradually; each in his degree; each in its proper place, in order
adj. & adv. According to the rank or degree (of)
adv. By many degrees, far, ever so much.

def: s.m. pl. (of darja), Steps, stages, degrees, &c.
s.m. A boy, son, child; a young animal.

ety: ḍar, q.v.+āk = Prk. आवको = S. आपकः (āpi, caus. aug.+aka)
def: adj. & s.m. Fearful, apprehensive, timorous, timid; — a timid person; a coward, dastard.
s.m. A tooth, tusk (see dāṛh).
s.m. = darkā, q.v.
s.m. A shield or buckler of solid leather; shield; coat of mail.
s.m. pl. (of darakat), Depths; stages downwards, descents (opp. to darajāt).

r: daraḵẖsḥān, duraḵẖshān, duraḵẖsh-īdan, darekhsh, dareś, darś
ety: fr. duraḵẖsh-īdan; fr. darekhsh = Zend. dareś; S. darś
def: part. Shining, flashing, brilliant.
part. adj. Shining, glittering, bright, lucid, refulgent; — s.m. Shiner, &c.
s.f. Brightness, brilliance, splendour.
s.f. Shining; splendour, &c. = next.
s.m. A tree.
s.m. = dhaṛakkā, dhaṛkā, qq.v.
s.m. (dialec.) Alarm, fright, terror.

ety: darak˚ = Prk. दरक्क(इ) or दरक्के(इ), fr. S. दर+कृ
def: v.n. To split, rend, crack, be torn ( = taṛaknā); — to palpitate, throb, beat.
v.n. To split, &c. ( = daraknā); — to open the mouth wide, to eat greedily.
adj. & s.m. Fearful, apprehensive, timorous, timid; — a timid person; a coward, dastard.
s.f. See s.v. dar, 'in.'

ety: act. part. of dar-īdan; see dar
def: part. adj. Rending, tearing (used in comp.).
s.m. The clearing-nut plant, Strychnos potatorum.

v.t. To caus. to fear, to make (one) afraid, to frighten, alarm, terrify, scare, intimidate, threaten, daunt, deter; — adj. (f. -ī), Causing fear or dread, fearful, frightful, dreadful, terrifying, terrible, formidable, tremendous; intimidating, threatening, deterrent

ety: act. part. of dar-īdan; and = Zend dar+añt; S. दर्, fr. दृ+अन्त्
def: adj. & s.m. Tearing, rending; rapacious, ferocious, ravenous; — a tearer, render; a rapacious or ravenous animal, a beast of prey (syn. dad).

ety: act. part. of dar-īdan; and = Zend dar+añt; S. दर्, fr. दृ+अन्त्
def: adj. & s.m. Tearing, rending; rapacious, ferocious, ravenous; — a tearer, render; a rapacious or ravenous animal, a beast of prey (syn. dad).
s.m. A hooked knife; a bill.
s.f. A kind of sickle, a reaping-hook; an instrument to cut grass with, a kind of scythe
adj. Afraid, apprehensive.
v.n. To fear, be afraid, &c. ( = ḍarnā, q.v.).
s.f. A crack, fissure, rift, rent, chasm, gap, opening, aperture, breach.
s.f. A crack, fissure, rift, rent, chasm, gap, opening, aperture, breach.
s.m.? (local), An impetuous flood; a torrent; a water-fall.
s.f. = H دڙاڙا दड़ाड़ा daṛāṛā, s.m. = darāṛ, q.v.
s.m. = H دڙاڙ दड़ाड़ daṛāṛ, s.f. = darāṛ, q.v.
v.n. To look (at), to see; to be visible, to appear, be seen.

ety: ḍar, q.v.+āʼu = āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. = ḍarānā or ḍarāʼonā, q.v.
adj. = ḍarāʼonā or ḍarānā, q.v.; — s.m. A timid person; a coward.
s.m. Frightening, intimidation; threat.
v.t. & adj. (f. -ī) = ḍarānā, q.v.
s.f. Drawers (of a chest, &c.).

ety: Pehl. drāj; Zend drājaṅh, rt. draj? 'to seize'; cf. S. dr̤ih and drāgh
def: adj. Long, tall, extended, stretched out
subs: v.t. To lengthen, extend, stretch out; to let loose, make free use of (the tongue)
adj. & s.m. Long-eared; an ass; a hare
v.n. To be lengthened, be extended; to stretch out, lie down full length.

def: s.f. Length; extension.

ety: dar = S. दर+bā = awā = Prk. अओ (with w inserted) = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A house or roost for fowls or pigeons, fowl-house; (ironic.) house, place
subs: intj. Into the house! in, in! to roost, to roost!

def: s.m. An intoxicating spirit made from rice.
s.m. See s.v. dar, 'door.'
s.m. See s.v. dar, 'door.'
s.f. See s.v. dar, 'value.'
s.m. A house, dwelling; court, area; hall of audience, court; holding of a court, a levee; royal audience; the executive government of a native state
subs: s.m. Court charges, political or diplomatic expenditure; charge for presents and gratuities made to princes and public functionaries, and for bribes, &c. (under the Mohammadan rule, an addition made to the assessment by government officers, or the zamīndārs, on the plea of providing for gratuities, exacted by their superiors or the state, on their payment of the revenue)
s.f. Presence or attendance at court
(-kī), To be assiduous at court; to dance attendance (on), to be obsequious
s.m. Public audience; public hall of audience
To hold a court or levee.

def: v.t. To represent as false; to misrepresent; — to intimidate, to threaten.

adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the court, court, courtly; attending court; — one who attends court, a courtier; — one entitled to a seat in a levee; — darbārī or darbārī zabān, s.f. Court language; the elegant and polished language of the court.
see s.v. dar, 'in.'

s.f. A spoon, ladle.
s.f. Cinnamon; bark of the Laurus cassia

ety: Pehl. dart; Zend drīta, fr. dar = S. dar
def: s.m. Pain, ache; affliction; pity, compassion, sympathy; affection

adj. Pained, grieved, overwhelmed in grief
adj. Painful, distressing; piteous, compassionate; feeling, pathetic
(-ko), To be affected with compassion, to feel pity
adj. Painful, &c. ( = dard-āmez); pitiable, exciting compassion, affecting; sickly
adj. Grieved, afflicted: dard-ě-zih, s.m. Pains or throes of labour
s.m. dard-sarī, s.f. Head-ache; labour, pains, trouble, vexation

s.m. Pain in the kidneys, nephritic pains

adj. Without sympathy, unsympathetic, unfeeling, pitiless, hard-hearted, cruel
s.f. Want of sympathy, unfeelingness, heartlessness, cruelty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian درد (dard), from Middle Persian dlt' (dard, “pain”).

def: s.m. The country of the Dāds (called Dārd-istān), bordering on Kashmīr and the mountains about Kashmīr and Peshāwar; — the inhabitants of this country, the Dārds.
intj. O the pain! alas! woe's me!
s.m. Cinnabar.
adj. (f. -ī), Broken, bruised, crushed, coarsely ground, half-pounded, granulated.

s.m. Cutaneous eruption, herpes ( = dadru; dād)

def: s.m. A frog; — a pipe or flute; — name of a mountain in the south (sometimes associated with the mountain Malaya).

s.m. Denial, refusal; repugnance, disinclination; regret, sorrow, vexation, grief; a sigh; — intj. alas! — dareg̠ rakhnā, dareg̠ karnā (-kā), To withhold (from, -se), be niggard or grudging (in respect of), to grudge, to think much (of)
subs: (with dat. of person, and abl. of thing), To have a disinclination or objection (e.g. mujhe kisī bāt-se dareg̠ nahīṅ).

def: intj. Alas! ah! well-a-day!
s.m. Impetuosity, violent attack; hard or pelting rain.
s.f. Muslin, printed muslin, any fine printed stuff.
adj. & s.m. Dressed; adjusted; put into exact line; — any very straight line; a road; margin; — adv. In a line, in order
subs: v.t. To dress; to put into exact line.

def: s.m. A dresser (of wounds, &c.); — s.f. The being in an exact line, evenness, regularity
subs: (-kī), To make level, or even, or straight (a road, &c.); to adjust, to put to rights.

def: s.f. Muslin, printed muslin, any fine printed stuff.
s.f. Portal, door; threshold; a royal court, a palace; a mosque; shrine or tomb (of some reputed saint, which is the object of worship and pilgrimage).
s.f. contrac. of dargāh, q.v. (used chiefly in poetry).
adj. Of, or belonging to, or connected with a dargāh (used at the end of compounds).
see s.v. dar, 'in.'

= P درگرفت dar-girift, See s.v. dar, 'in.'

v.n. To grind or gnash the teeth in wrath or anguish, &c.

def: s.f. A jaw-tooth, grinder

To gnash the teeth and weep, to cry aloud or bitterly
To grind or gnash the teeth (from rage, despair, or grief, &c.).

def: adj. Beard-shaven; beardless.

def: s.f. Confusion, disorder.

s.f. Beard, whiskers ( = dāṛhī)

To let the beard grow, to keep a beard
To pluck out every single hair of a beard
To pull or pluck out the beard

def: s.f. Beard ( = ḍāṛhī); awn; tendril
subs: lit. 'To whiten (one's) beard in the sun'; to be wanting in experience, or wisdom, or judgment

def: adj. Bearded, having a long beard.
adj. Having a beard, bearded; having a long beard.
adj. Having a beard, bearded; having a long beard.
adj. See s.v. daṛh.
adj. Having the beard cropped, beard-sbaven, beardless.

def: s.f. = dāṛhī, q.v.
s.f. A natural or artificial excavation in a mountain, a cave, cavern, grotto; a valley ( = darra).
s.f. A carpet (made of cotton); a kind of cotton cloth (resembling drill, made in India).
s.f. = ḍālī, q.v.
s.f. Having, possessing, holding, &c. (used in comp., e.g. jahān-dārī).
s.f. Rending, tearing (used in comp.).

adj. Of or pertaining to a valley or glen, living or found in valleys or hills (e.g. kabk-ě-darī, q.v.s.v. kabk); — s.f. (fr. dara, or fr. dar, 'gate'; 'palace'), Name of one of the three surviving dialects of the seven anciently spoken in Persia (said by some to be the dialect of a certain district comprehending Balkh, Bukhārā, Badakhshān, Marū-Shāh-Jahān, Bāmiyān, and other mountainous parts; and by others to have been spoken at the court of the monarchs of the Kaiānian dynasty, and called the language of the court and of paradise).

ety: dar = S. दर+ebā = ewā = Prk. इअओ (with w inserted) = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A stall (in a market) where betel is sold (cf. daṛbā).

ety: dar, q.v.+dim. aff.-īća = ća = ak = S. क
def: s.m. A window, casement; a small hole for the admission of light; — a wicket.
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of darīdan; and = Zend dareta, rt. dar = S. dar, rt. dr̤ī
def: part. adj. Torn, rent
subs: adj. Open-mouthed; plain-spoken, saying whatever comes uppermost.

def: s.f. Rent, tear, fissure; laceration.
s.m. Poverty, indigence, wretchedness
subs: 'Driving out poverty'; a superstition or religious ceremony observed on the morning of the Dīwālī festival (a sieve or winnowing basket is beaten in every corner of the house, with the words īshvar paiṭho, dāridr niklo, 'Enter, O God; depart, O Poverty').
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit दारिद्र (dāridra). Doublet of دارِد (dārid).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Poor, needy, indigent, distressed, wretched ( = dāridr); — a poor or needy person; — poverty, indigence.

s.m. = S دردرتا दरिद्रता daridratā, s.f. Poverty, indigence, penury, wretchedness.
adj. Poor, indigent, needy, unfortunate, wretched.
adj. contrac. of dāridrī, q.v.
s.f. A poor or wretched woman.
s.f. A poor or wretched woman.
s.m. = dāridr, q.v.
s.f. A daughter, girl
subs: s.m. The giving of a daughter in marriage.

def: s.m. The pomegranate (fruit or tree).

v.t. To fold (in, -meṅ); to enter, insert; to include; to copy (into), to write (in), to book, to register, to record

def: s.f. A dozen.
s.m. corr. of darzī, q.v.

ety: v.n. of an unused form درك; see idrāk

s.m. Rent, fissure, breach, crack, crevice, cranny.
v.t. To cause to crack or split, &c.; to split, rend, tear, &c.

ety: dar, 'in,' q.v.+Zend kāra = S. kāra, or kārya
def: adj. Necessary; in requisition, required, wanted.
adv. In fear, in alarm, through dread, timidly, terrified.
adj. Needful, requisite.
adv. In fear, in alarm, through dread, timidly, terrified.

subs: (-ke), To come between, to intervene; to take place between; to interrupt, to obtrude
(-ko), To put between (as a sacred book, for the purpose of deciding or adjusting), to employ as a mediator, to propose as an umpire, or a security; to give as a hostage
adj. (f. -kī), Of or pertaining to the middle, midmost, &c. ( = darmiyānī, q.v.)

adv. In the interval or interim, in the meantime, meanwhile.

def: adj. & s.m. Midmost, intermediate, intervening; middling, fair; interior, inner; included, contained; — a mediator, middle-man, go-between; an interpreter (syn. mutarjim).
v.t. = ḍālnā, q.v.

ety: ḍar˚ = Prk. डर(इ), fr. डर = S. दर
def: v.n. To be afraid (of, -se), be frightened (by, -se), be daunted; to fear, dread (with abl. case)
adv. See s.v. dar, 'in.'

ety: old P. darauga; Pehl. darog; Zend draogha, fr. druj = S. drogha, rt. druh
def: adj. & s.m. Lying, false; — a lie, falsehood (syn. jhūṭ)

adj. & s.m. False, lying; — a liar.
s.f. Falsehood ( = darog̠).

ety: prob. fr. S. द्रा or द्राव, rt. द्रु

(-se), To take a lesson (from), to read a lesson

def: s.m. Seeing; sight, &c. ( = darś, q.v.)

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَرْس (dars), from Arabic دَرْس (dars).

def: s.m. Seeing, sight, view, look, appearance; — the moon when only just becoming visible, the new moon; the day of new moon; the conjunction of the sun and moon

def: s.m. = darśak, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Seeing, looking at; examining, searching; causing to be perceived, showing, displaying, exhibiting, explaining; — a spectator; an examiner, a searcher; who, or what, shows or displays, or explains or makes clear, a shower, an indicator, &c.
s.m. = darśan, q.v.

s.m. Seeing, looking, observing; sight, vision, observation, look, view; appearance, aspect, semblance; perception; exhibition; inspection, examination; going into the presence of, visiting, an interview; visiting a sacred shrine, worshipping in the presence of an image; — a view or theory prescribed in a system or book; one of the six religious or philosophical systems of the Hindūs, a śāstra; — mental or spiritual vision, contemplation; a vision, dream; — apprehension, judgment, opinion; discernment, understanding, intellect
subs: (-kā), To obtain a sight (of), to be admitted to an interview (with)
s.m. The advantage gained by an interview; the blessing arising out of a visit to a shrine
s.m. Surety for appearance, sponsor, bail
(-ko), To appear before (a person), put in an appearance; to grant or vouchsafe an interview
v.t. To visit, have an interview; to pay a visit to a shrine
(˚na+in˚), s.m. The organ of sight, the eye; the sense of sight.

def: v.t. = darśānā, q.v.
v.t. To show, cause to be seen, to exhibit.
adj. = darśanī, q.v.

adj. To be seen, worthy of being seen, good-looking, beautiful, handsome, comely, sightly; — payable at sight, at sight (a bill of exchange); — s.f. A present at an interview; a present, an offering; a bill of exchange payable at sight; — an epithet of the goddess Durgā
subs: s.m. A youth worthy of being seen, a beautiful youth
s.f. A bill of exchange, or a money order, payable at sight.

def: adj. = darśanīya, q.v.
adj. Worthy of being seen, beautiful, &c. (see darśanī)

def: part. = darśāyā, q.v.

adj. = darśī, q.v.
adj. Seeing, looking, observing; examining, inspecting; knowing; showing, explaining, &c. (com. used as the last member of compounds).
adj. Relating to reading or study, relating to a prescribed course of study.
part. Shown, displayed, exhibited, exposed to view; explained; seen, understood; visible, apparent.
part., corr. of darśit, q.v.

def: part. adj. Fearing, fearful, apprehensive

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَارُو (dārū, “medicine, drug, gunpowder”), itself derived from Middle Persian dʾlwkˈ (dārūg, “drug, medicament”).

dāruj, adj. Produced from wood, made of wood, wooden
adj. Abounding in wood, consisting of wood, woody; wooden

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَارُو (dārū, “medicine, drug, gunpowder”), itself derived from Middle Persian dʾlwkˈ (dārūg, “drug, medicament”).

ety: v.n. of darūdan = darīdan; rt. Zend dar = S. dar, fr. dr̤ī

v.n. To bellow (as cattle), &c. ( = daḍūknā, q.v.).
s.m. Inside, interior; — heart; bowels; — adv. In, within; inwards.

adj. & s.m. Hard, harsh, rough, severe (as speech, &c.), austere; cruel, pitiless, heart-rending; terrible, terrific, frightful, fearful, dreadful, shocking; agonizing, painful; intense, violent; — harshness, sevevity, hard-heartedness, cruelty; horror, horribleness; — the plant lead-wort, Plumbago zeylanica
subs: (˚na+ari), s.m. 'Enemy of cruelty,' an epithet of God, or of any merciful being.

def: s.m. The inside, interior; the heart, core.
adj. Internal, interior, inner; intrinsic.

ety: darū+ṛā and ṛī = Prk. अडओ and अडिआ = S. र+कः and इका
def: Wine; spirituous liquor ( = dārū).

A door, or door-way, to be built up with bricks and mortar
To wear away the ground about a door (by frequent visiting); to visit (a place) frequently

def: s.m. Keeper of a gate (of a town), a warden.

r: darvesh, driwis, driwi, dareghusćiṭ, drighu, drigh, darbh, dr̤ibh, daridra
ety: fr. Zend driwis, nom. of driwi; or fr. dareghusćiṭ, nom. of drighu; rt. drigh, 'to be poor, to beg'; cf. S. darbh, rt. dr̤ibh; and daridra
def: adj. & s.m. Poor, indigent; — a dervish, a religious mendicant, a beggar (pl. darveshān)

ety: old P. daraya, daryāw; Pehl. zraē; Zend zrayaṅh, rt. zri = S. jrayas, rt. jri

s.m. 'The salt waters,' the sea, ocean

'To enclose the sea in a jar'; to say much in a few words, to be concise or comprehensive; — to attempt the impossible.

ety: daryā, q.v.+dim. aff. -ća = ak = S. क

def: s.f. corr. of dārāʼī, q.v.

ety: fr. daryūz = daryūsh or daryosh = darwesh, q.v.
def: s.m. Begging, &c. ( = darweza, q.v.).

ety: Zend darez; S. darh, rt. dr̤iṅh
def: s.f.m.(?) Opening, breach, rent, fissure, cleft, crack, flaw; seam, suture (of a garment, &c.); sewing; a rent (in a garment) that has been sewn up; — a rag, slip (of cloth), long strip (of cloth), a narrow shred

ety: fem. of darzī; ˚an or ˚in = S. inī
